title: Evangelion The Endless Debate Fanime 2009 Panel
display_title: Evangelion The Endless Debate Fanime 2009 Panel
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion: The Endless Debate (Fanime 2009 Panel)             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun May 24, 2009 1:57 pm It was rather difficult to decide which subforum was most appropriate for holding this topic. I eventually decided on this one, but I'm not picky. Move it if doing so seems appropriate. I recorded... some of it... but even though my camera was fully charged, it ran out of batteries way faster than seemed possible. I'll upload what I recorded when I get back home. For now, here are some interesting things I remember, in chronological (sort of) order: -Carl Horn and Sean McCoy seem to be rather good friends. -I asked Sean before the panel started if he had heard of the CI and NGEs2 . He hadn't, and asked me how well it meshed with his own beliefs. I told him that he could find it at wiki.evageeks.org; don't yell at me if he shows up, though, as Carl mentioned Evageeks a couple of times during the panel as well. -Sean started the panel by apologizing for Mythology of Evangelion, which unfortunately I did not get on camera. He did, however, reiterate several things he said in Mythology throughout the panel, such as Sachiel's rib symbolizing woman and all that. -Sean was a lurker at evageeks.org back when it was called such and is well aware of how much hatred is directed towards him, though he still stands by his statement that Shinji and Asuka had sex. -At least three other people in the audience were familiar with Re-Take . Carl is familiar with Studio Kimigabuchi and met the writer of Re-Take at least once due to a Gunbuster doujin he wrote. -Sean is a big ol' David Lynch fan. He brought up Twin Peaks several times, and credits his understanding of Twin Peaks as one of the things that helped him understand NGE. -Sean said he likes 1.0 because, according to him, it fits so perfectly with what he said in Mythology. I asked him later about how he feels about Shamshel no longer looking phallic, and he insisted that Shamshel still looks "plenty phallic". - The Prisoner was brought up several times. Both Carl and Sean feel that The Prisoner 's final two episodes were very influential on Eva 's. -I asked Sean whose soul he thought was in Unit 00, and he changed the subject somewhat to the fact that there aren't any living mothers anywhere in NGE (which some people in the audience went "Ooohhh" at). There was no actual "endless debate" on this or anything else; Carl said that that sort of question was a good thing to bring up at evageeks.org. -Carl confirmed that Robin Williams did indeed audition to play Gendo in the dub and that his audition recording still exists somewhere. [skipping a lot of stuff I'll probably remember later] -After the panel was over, Sean stayed around for a while answering questions posed to him by a small horde. I asked him if he felt a LAEM director could ever do as good a job as Anno, and he said he doesn't think LAEM will ever happen, despite Greenfield's insistence. I also asked if he feels Gainax is moving more towards typical commercial works and away from experimental art, and he said he doesn't believe Gainax's animations have ever been anything but comercial. I'll say some more after I get back from getting foodstuffs.

[]:             Quote Post by The Invincible Shinji » Sun May 24, 2009 2:30 pm Damn thats pretty awesome. And Robin Willams auditioning to play as Gendo was a bit of a suprise but i've heard he's a huge Eva fan, and its possible Asuka and Shinji had sex ( i'm gonna get my butt kicked for that ). Well anyway, i can't wait to see the recording.

[]: Re: Evangelion: The Endless Debate (Fanime 2009 Panel)             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun May 24, 2009 2:51 pm Who the fuck is Sean McCoy, and why do we care what he thinks about Eva? Did he help create the series in the same way that Matt Greenfield did? NAveryW wrote: -Carl confirmed that Robin Williams did indeed audition to play Gendo in the dub and that his audition recording still exists somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun May 24, 2009 2:57 pm Back from brunch. I remembered a couple more things Carl said that aren't really Evangelion -related, but are still pretty interesting. -Despite the fact that (as far as I remember), Anno was not credited for it, he wrote much of the final episode of Diebuster . This may be common knowledge that I missed before, as Carl said this was from a Bandai booklet that came with the DVD, but I felt I'd post it here because it was the first I'd heard of it. -Carl asked Yamaga at one point whether Mahoromatic 's target demographic was really 14-year-olds. Yamaga replied that it's more intended for 41-year-olds, which, now that I think about it, makes the fact that it's crammed full of loli nudity/fanservice a bit creepy. Just a bit. -Dark Horse gets its share of weird letters. One person insisted that he wrote DBZ and Ranma 1/2 and that the credited writers stole them from him. Another person wrote a long essay about why he loved Rei, giving no indication he didn't realize she wasn't real. Another was a message in Swedish printed diagonally across a sheet of paper without regard for the fact that some of the message was being cut off. BrikHaus wrote: Who the fuck is Sean McCoy, and why do we care what he thinks about Eva? Did he help create the series in the same way that Matt Greenfield did?

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Sun Jun 07, 2009 8:06 pm Thanks to everyone who attended the aptly-named "Evangelion: The Endless Debate" panel. I apologize if I promoted a misunderstanding, but I'm not sure that Robin Williams ever auditioned for Evangelion per se. What I do believe to be true is that he lent his voice (for Fuyutsuki, not Gendo) to a demo dub of Episode 1 as a favor to an acquaintance who was trying to break into audio post-production. This was, I believe, around 1996 or 1997. It's not clear to me whether either Williams or the acquaintance were actually trying to get involved in Evangelion --it may have been simply that Eva was being used as the demo because it was something new with buzz at the time. It would explain how Robin Williams came to know about the series, however.
